title: .to_string() on &String should be .clone()
description: In lib.rs, the compile() function constructs SieveErrorKind::UnsupportedExtension(s.to_string()) where s is a &String (destructured from Form::Str(s)). Calling .to_string() on a &String goes through the Display machinery to produce a new String — semantically identical to .clone() but roundabout. Clippy lint clippy::implicit_clone flags this pattern. The idiomatic form is s.clone(). Same pattern appears twice: once in the Form::Str arm (line ~148) and once in the Form::StringList loop (line ~158) of the require extension validation loop.
